Mahmoud Mohamed is a Python Developer with contact information including email (mahmoud.mohamed@email.com), phone number (+20 102 345 6789), and links to his GitHub (github.com/mahmoud-mohamed) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/mahmoud-mohamed) profiles, as well as a personal website (mahmoudmohamed.com).

Mahmoud Mohamed received a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from Savitribai Phule Pune University in June 2018, and completed his HSC in June 2014 and SSC in June 2012 from the Maharashtra State Board.

From July 2018 to October 2019, Mahmoud Mohamed worked as a Python Developer at Atos Syntel, focusing on computer vision for a US-based banking client. He designed and developed computer vision algorithms using OpenCV, PIL, and NumPy, and utilized Git for version control and debugging.

Mahmoud Mohamed participated in several projects including KPIT-Sparkle Project (2018), Accenture Innovation Project (2018), and Smart Ambulance System (2017), achieving 1st Rank for the poster presentation of the Smart Ambulance System at the college level.

Mahmoud Mohamed completed several certificates including ESD Program (Zensar Technologies, Pune, 2017), Employability Training (Barclays, Global Talent Track, and NASSCOM Foundation, 2015), Medical IoT Workshop (IEEE Standard’s Association, Pune, 2017), Introduction to Arduino Workshop (SCOE, Pune, 2016), and Robotics for Juniors Workshop (Computer Society of India, Pune, 2016).

Mahmoud Mohamed received the 1st Rank for a college-level poster presentation on the Smart Ambulance System in Pune in 2017 and his project idea was selected for the final prototyping round in the KPIT-Sparkle competition in Pune in 2018.

Mahmoud Mohamed possesses skills in Programming (Python), Databases (MySQL, General Database Concepts), Frameworks/Libraries (Django, OpenCV, PIL, NumPy), Web Technologies (HTML5, REST API), and Tools & Version Control (Git).

Mahmoud Mohamed held leadership roles in extracurricular activities, serving as Treasurer (2017-2018) for the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE in Pune and as Team Leader (2016-2017) for College Technical and Cultural Events.

Mahmoud Mohamed organized an IEEE workshop on ‘Excellence in English and Public Speaking’ in Pune in 2017, coordinated sponsorship of Rs. 15,000 from Platinum Auto (formerly Royal Enfield) in Pune in 2017, and participated in Avishkar at Savitribai Phule Pune University.
Mahmoud Mohamed actively participated in multiple inter-college technical competitions across Pune at various colleges including SCOE, PICT, and AISSMS in 2016-2017.

Pune emerges as a vibrant hub of educational and technological ambition, a city deeply woven into the fabric of Mahmoud Mohamed’s formative years. It’s a place where academic foundations are laid at Savitribai Phule Pune University, the source of Mahmoud’s Bachelor of Engineering, and reinforced by the Maharashtra State Board’s secondary education system. More than just a location, Pune actively *shaped* Mahmoud’s skillset. It’s within its tech centers – Zensar Technologies, Barclays through Global Talent Track and NASSCOM Foundation – that he honed practical skills through programs like ESD and Employability Training. 

The city fostered his curiosity, hosting workshops on cutting-edge fields like Medical IoT (IEEE Standards Association) and robotics (Computer Society of India, SCOE), and providing a platform for hands-on learning with Arduino. Pune isn’t merely a backdrop for achievement; it *recognizes* it. Mahmoud’s innovative “Smart Ambulance System” earned him first place in a college poster presentation, and his project gained traction in the competitive KPIT-Sparkle arena. 

This is a Pune that encourages leadership, evidenced by Mahmoud’s roles within the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE and his direction of college events. It’s a city that supports initiative, allowing him to organize workshops and secure sponsorships from local businesses like Platinum Auto.  Pune’s collegiate landscape – SCOE, PICT, AISSMS, and of course, Savitribai Phule Pune University itself – became a proving ground, a network of inter-college competitions fueling his drive. Ultimately, Pune isn’t just *where* Mahmoud learned and grew, but a key ingredient *in* that growth, a city that actively participates in nurturing talent and innovation. It resides within the larger state of Maharashtra, yet possesses a distinct energy, a breeding ground for aspiring engineers and leaders.

Mahmoud Mohamed is a driven Python Developer, readily connecting with the world through mahmoud.mohamed@email.com, +20 102 345 6789, and online profiles at github.com/mahmoud-mohamed and linkedin.com/in/mahmoud-mohamed, further showcasing his work at mahmoudmohamed.com. A 2018 graduate of Savitribai Phule Pune University with a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science, his foundation was built through earlier studies with the Maharashtra State Board.

His professional journey began at Atos Syntel, where he spent over a year honing his skills in computer vision, crafting algorithms with OpenCV, PIL, and NumPy for a US banking client, and mastering version control with Git. Beyond this role, Mahmoud consistently sought opportunities to expand his expertise, completing certifications in areas like Medical IoT, Arduino, and Robotics. He demonstrated a knack for innovation early on, achieving first rank for a poster presentation on his ‘Smart Ambulance System’ – a project that also earned recognition in the KPIT-Sparkle competition. 

Mahmoud isn’t solely defined by technical prowess; he’s a natural leader, having served as Treasurer for the IEEE Student Branch and Team Leader for college events. He proactively organized workshops, secured sponsorships, and actively participated in numerous inter-college technical competitions, demonstrating a commitment to both personal growth and community engagement. His skillset encompasses Python programming, database management (MySQL), web technologies (HTML5, REST APIs), and a strong command of frameworks like Django. He’s a builder, a learner, and a collaborator – a young engineer with a clear trajectory and a proven ability to deliver.
